title: Tutorial for Computer Programming I
summary: A structured tutorial sequence on Python fundamentals, control flow, functions, loops, and list-based problem solving.
outcome: Students can solve beginner Python problems independently using conditionals, loops, functions, and list-processing patterns.

schedule.topic: Getting started: variables, basic I/O, and expressions
Numbers, variables, typed input, and arithmetic workflows
Boolean logic, operators, and nested conditional decision-making
Function decomposition, loops, and core numeric problem patterns
Lists, built-in list methods, and custom list-processing functions

notebooks.title: Getting started: variables, basic I/O, and expressions
Print formatting in depth: sep, end, newline, and output control
Numbers and understanding data through structured output
Variable input, type-casting, and arithmetic workflows
Boolean logic and arithmetic operators with truth-table reasoning
Basic function decomposition and extended conditional problem-solving
Loops: numeric series, factorial, prime tests, and Fibonacci
Lists with loops and core list built-in functions
Custom list-processing functions and integrated practice

title: Tutorial for Computer Programming II
summary: Intermediate Python tutorial covering nested conditionals, loops, functions, recursion, built-in data structures (List, Tuple, Set, Dictionary), modularization, file I/O, exception handling, and object-oriented programming.
outcome: Students can formulate and implement solutions to real-life engineering problems using advanced Python — applying OOP principles (class, object, methods, inheritance, polymorphism), built-in data structures, recursion, exception handling, and file I/O to build comprehensive, well-structured programs.

schedule.topic: Conditional statements (nested): if-else, if-elif-else, nested if
Loops (nested): while, for, nested loops, break, continue, pass
Functions: calls, parameters, scope of variables, generators
Built-in data types: List and 2D List
Built-in types: List, Tuple, Set, Dictionary

notebooks.title: Built-in data types: List and 2D List

title: Tutorial for Data Structure
summary: Tutorial sessions on data representation and storage using elementary data structures — arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, heaps, and graphs — with implementation and problem solving in Python.
outcome: Students can implement and use core data structures (linked lists, stacks, queues, BSTs, heaps, graphs, hash tables), analyze time/space complexity, apply recursion to algorithmic problem solving, and select appropriate data structures for engineering problems.

schedule.topic: Python review: loops, conditionals, functions, lists, tuples, sets, dicts, classes
Pointers, nodes, and singly linked list
Stack and queue using linked list
Binary search tree — operations and complexity
Binary search tree — traversal and applications

notebooks.title: Python review: data types, control flow, functions, OOP
Pointers, nodes, and singly linked list
Stack and queue using linked list
Recursion: base cases, call stack, and recursive problem solving
Binary search tree: insertion, deletion, search

resources.title: Problem Solving with Algorithms and Data Structures using Python — Miller & Ranum
Python Data Structures and Algorithms — Benjamin Baka
Data Structures and Algorithms in Python — Goodrich, Tamassia & Goldwasser

title: Fundamentals of Computer Systems
summary: Tutorial sessions covering computer-system concepts and practical office-suite skills for academic and professional use.
outcome: Students understand fundamental computing concepts and can use office productivity tools effectively for academic reporting and presentations.

schedule.topic: Microsoft Word: formatting and document design
Microsoft Excel: formulas, charts, and data analysis

title: Numerical Methods Lab
summary: Lab worksheets and assignments for numerical methods — root-finding, interpolation, numerical integration, and ODEs — implemented in Python.
outcome: Students can implement and analyze core numerical methods, understand their error characteristics, and apply them to scientific computing problems.

schedule.topic: Floating point arithmetic and error analysis
Linear systems: Gaussian elimination and LU factorization
Ordinary differential equations: Euler and Runge-Kutta

notebooks.title: Numerical integration: trapezoidal and Simpson's rule

resources.title: Numerical Methods for Engineers — Chapra & Canale
